name: Dark Pumpernickel Bread
recipeInstructions:
Preparation Time: 0:45 minutes
1. Dissolve yeast in warm water in a large mixing bowl.
2. Stir in molasses, caraway seeds, salt, shortening, rye flour, 1/4 cup cornmeal, and cocoa.
3. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until smooth.
4. Stir in enough all-purpose flour to make a stiff dough.
5. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Cover and let rest 10 to 15 minutes.
6. Knead until smooth and elastic (5 to 10 minutes).
7. Shape dough into a ball, and place in a well-greased bowl, turning to grease the top.
8. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85°F), free from drafts, 1 hour or until doubled in bulk.
9. Punch dough down; shape into a ball. Cover and let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, about 40 minutes or until doubled in bulk.
10. Grease a baking sheet, and sprinkle with cornmeal.
11. Punch dough down, and divide in half. Shape each half into a round, slightly flat loaf.
12. Place loaves on opposite corners of the baking sheet. Cover and let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, 1 hour.
13. Bake at 375°F for 25 to 30 minutes or until loaves sound hollow when tapped.
14. Remove from baking sheet, and let cool on wire racks.
Yield: 2 loaves
